title: Creating forms for subitems?
excerpt:
Does anyone know if there is currently a way to create forms to populate subitem fields? I’ve tried to search similar topics here but couldn’t find any answers. 
I’m trying to avoid creating multiple boards so I can stor…

posts.author: northstarjojo (Joanne Huang)
posts.cookedHtml:
Does anyone know if there is currently a way to create forms to populate subitem fields? I’ve tried to search similar topics here but couldn’t find any answers.
I’m trying to avoid creating multiple boards so I can store all the information I need on one board. Subitems are good but they require our team to fill out all the info instead of sending forms to our potential clients and have them fill out the info themselves.

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @northstarjojo, welcome to the community and thanks for posting!

At the moment forms can’t populate subitem fields. I would try dividing the board into groups and making extensive use of the filters so that you can store all the information you need in one board:

  • The Board Filters

posts.author: danhall76 (Daniel Clarke-Hall)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there any functionality in Monday that is actually finished? Everything seems half baked. I’ve never seen so many ‘workarounds’ in my life.

Do you know when functionality to add subitems to forms will be added?

posts.author: tommy_H (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any update on when this will ever happen @TRB-monday.com ?
@danhall76 you are spot on! I’m constantly having to find workarounds.
Did you find one with subitems and forms? I’m about to check jotforms as a solution.
Unfortunately subitems a must for me and this form limitation is another slap in the face.
The roadmap has a mention of form v2 upgrade but that doesn’t even have a time of completion and it may not even include subitems. Perhaps @TRB-monday.com can update us?

posts.author: tommy_H (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml: @TRB-monday.com Just checking if you are checking this community? Left a question a few weeks ago

posts.author: bmillner (T Bradley Millner)
posts.cookedHtml: This functionality has the potential to be HUGE. I have to use JotForms instead

posts.author: Or.Amir (Or Amir)
posts.cookedHtml: Following. This function is definitely overdue.

posts.author: gbarker121 (Geoffrey Barker)
posts.cookedHtml: Another upvote for this functionality!

posts.author: LCFC_HK (Hollie Kinch)
posts.cookedHtml: This function would be a massive step forward, would definitely vote for this! Any chance this would be coming in the near future?

posts.author: JoochenC (Jochen Czwalina)
posts.cookedHtml: Would make use of it too, following …

posts.author: EthanPenn (Ethan Dale)
posts.cookedHtml: Between the mobile app, subitems, and many other things, it seems entertaining that every feature released is at about 10% functionality. The platform would look much better if you released single features at 90% functionality, one by one, and then finished it from there.

posts.author: kate.bartley (Kate Bartley)
posts.cookedHtml: Would love this feature to be added!

posts.author: lizpfahler (Elizabeth Pfahler)
posts.cookedHtml:
I added a new post in the Feedback section for this idea, please go and vote for it!.
We would like to use Monday for expense reimbursement but this is preventing us from doing that.

Request for ability to enter sub Items on a form Feature requests
Hello! We would love to use Monday for our Expense Reimbursements and get rid of the spreadsheets, but right now there doesn’t appear to be a way to create a new item via form and also break out sub-items. The need is to be able to list all the individual expenses and have only totals on the main item. Sub items inlude expense type, amount etc. Thank for you consideration! [image] There’s a conversation on this topic, but not a feedback entry so I thought I’d add one

posts.author: fureverafter (Michelle)
posts.cookedHtml: Can’t believe this is being so grossly overlooked by the Monday team. It’s a huge sore point for me and I’m reconsidering where to even keep using Monday.com at all. Other little things I can forgive, but this is a massive time killer for our business, and having to pay for multiple other programs to find a work-around that only partially even works is a total waste of time and money. Very disappointing.

posts.author: BizFocusedHarry (Harry)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a massive requirement for us. We could run $100,000s of revenue through a single board if this feature existed

posts.author: Michael-PT (Michael Albers)
posts.cookedHtml:
Definitely a great addition if it this feature would exist. Going to vote for feature request of @lizpfahler

Another option could be an automation: “when item created, convert item to subitem of item where this column matches this column in another group.” Probably need to flesh it out a bit better, but something like that.

posts.author: jjohnson86 (Jessica Johnson)
posts.cookedHtml:
There is an existing feature request, maybe if enough of us vote on it, they’ll look into this more?

Adding subitems to form Feature requests
Adding subitems using a form We are adding projects (items) in MONDAY using forms. When we have updates / changes on a project, we would like to handle it as subitems. We would like our clients to add subitems from a form. Can that be done? And how does the subitem get matched with the correct item?

posts.author: jpnonnet (jeanpierre.nonnet@photon.com)
posts.cookedHtml: Yeah @tommy_H , I have had the same experience every time I am faced with a nuanced request I have to constantly find workarounds in Monday.com it is starting to feel like monday.com was not built for enterprises but more small to mid size businesses with simple workflows. Which is unfortunate, enabling subitems in the form would be such a value add.

posts.author: mihai.oprisenescu (Mihai Oprisenescu)
posts.cookedHtml: did you found the solution? i have the same question also…

posts.author: mihai.oprisenescu (Mihai Oprisenescu)
posts.cookedHtml: strong text

posts.author: JoseV (Jose )
posts.cookedHtml:
Or Amir:

This function is definitely overdue.

@BiancaT Hello Bianca! I would like to ask you if monday are planning on to create this functionality or if there is any alternative way to do it at this time, that would be too useful for everyone! (:

Thanks!

posts.author: SourceImpactfully (Amanda)
posts.cookedHtml: +1! This would be very helpful to add.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @jpnonnet, @mihai.oprisenescu, @JoseV, @SourceImpactfully

Hey

We developed an app called Google Forms Integration Plus that allows getting responses from Google Forms directly monday.com.
Google Forms is free to use and has advanced capabilities that are missing in monday’s forms, and our app offers more flexible and robust logic. It basically solves all the gaps in monday’s forms.

You can easily create subitems based on responses from forms. Here are 2 examples.

Creating on a specific item →

Screenshot 2024-10-02 at 16.15.181820×280 16.4 KB

Creating on a dynamic item (based on the response in the form) →

Screenshot 2024-10-02 at 16.15.582084×348 32 KB

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - Google Forms Integration Plus - monday.com App Marketplace

Hope that helps!

posts.author: mcoleman84 (Megan Coleman)
posts.cookedHtml: Has anyone had luck with this? I have my sales reps out taking orders for the new year and it would be so much simpler if Cookie Monster’s order was one item, with each product as a separate subitem.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @mcoleman84 please see my reply above.

posts.author: leonidas (Leonidas)
posts.cookedHtml: Sub items via form submission feature release announced! I don’t see any specific release date but I suspect it should be out within the next couple of months.
